input.prompt:
I have two ASP.NET Core 8 services communicating via RabbitMQ. Service A publishes
an order event and Service B consumes it. Traces show up fine within each service
but I can't see the end-to-end distributed trace across the queue boundary.
How do I propagate the trace context through message headers? Don't create files.

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about propagating distributed trace context (OpenTelemetry/W3C Trace Context) across RabbitMQ message boundaries in ASP.NET Core 8. They want to see end-to-end distributed traces across Service A (publisher) and Service B (consumer). They don't want me to create files — just explain the approach.

This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code task. I'll provide a concise but helpful answer.
